mapping.a_element: 2D array of coupled superconducting units (Josephson junction array)
mapping.b_element: 2D triangular lattice of coupled spin-1/2 units (trapped 9Be+ ions)

mapping.a_element: external tuning knob (gate voltage / Fermi energy in graphene)
mapping.b_element: external tuning knob (optical dipole force setting the interaction exponent a)

brief_justification: The only overlap is generic subject matter (2D quantum many-body lattices, condensed-matter/superconductivity context) with an externally tunable control parameter; the governing machinery differs. Paper A is driven by a dissipation-induced superconductor-to-insulator quantum phase transition set by the competition of the Josephson effect, Coulomb blockade, and gapless-substrate dissipation, whereas Paper B engineers a tunable-range Ising Hamiltonian (J_ij ~ 1/d^a) via a spin-dependent optical dipole force for analog quantum simulation — no role-for-role mapping of a shared driving process holds.
